expectations.text: Review does NOT include a complexity/split advisory
expectations.evidence: The 93-line review contains no complexity advisory or suggestion to split the PR.

expectations.text: Review does not flag breaking changes
expectations.evidence: No mention of 'breaking' anywhere in the review.

The verdict states: 'the missing root-tab view context in the router-based element is a functional bug that means validation badges will not appear on the Generic tab for non-inline block editing. This should be fixed before merging.' While it doesn't use the exact phrase 'Request Changes', the directive to fix before merging is functionally equivalent.
The review is 93 lines, well under the 200-line threshold.

expectations.text: Review does not flag purely cosmetic/stylistic issues (formatting, whitespace, naming conventions, comment grammar, code style preferences) unless they affect performance or rendering. Missing JSDoc on new public APIs is NOT a stylistic issue -- it is a legitimate finding.
expectations.evidence: No stylistic or cosmetic findings. The observations about name parameter nullability, as any cast, and code duplication are all about correctness and maintainability, not style.

eval_feedback.overall: Clean pass on all assertions. The code_review_skill review is focused, proportionate, and correctly identifies the functional bug.
